guidance.use: Use for choosing one or more already-authorized media records from project-supplied results with accessible search, pagination, loading, and error states.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when a native file input is enough, when users must upload new files only, or when Composer would need direct storage credentials or library-query authority.
guidance.responsive: Keep the dialog, search, results, pagination, and close controls operable with touch and keyboard at narrow widths.

decision.nativeAlternative: FileUpload is simpler for choosing a new local file without an existing asset library.
decision.complexAlternative: Use a project media manager when foldering, transforms, permissions, destructive actions, metadata editing, or provider querying belong in the surface.
decision.stateModel: Selection, query, and page each support controlled or initial-default state; dialog visibility is internal.
decision.serverClientBoundary: Client boundary required for dialog, command search, selection, and pagination callbacks; media records must be serializable.
decision.serialization: A name emits selected media IDs as repeated hidden fields; URLs and metadata are presentation data, not persistence authority.
decision.focusOwnership: The dialog traps focus and returns it to the picker trigger; results must retain stable IDs during query and pagination updates.
failureModes: Passing unauthorized media records exposes assets the user should not discover.
Treating a selected URL as a trusted storage identifier permits confused-deputy writes.
Remote search without stable loading and error state causes duplicate or stale selection.

items.description: Already-authorized serializable media descriptors; Composer does not fetch, filter permissions, or sign URLs.

value.description: Controlled selected media IDs. Pair with onValueChange; defaultValue initializes local selection.

onLoadMore.description: Project callback for fetching more authorized results; Composer has no network or storage credentials.

runtime.listenerModel: Dialog focus management, command keyboard search, selection, pagination, and optional load-more callbacks.
runtime.scaleGuidance: Page or incrementally load large libraries and keep result IDs stable; do not send the entire media catalog to the client.

security.htmlPolicy: Titles, alternate text, metadata nodes, and errors render through React; project-supplied metadata nodes remain project-owned.
security.clientValidation: Client filtering and disabled items do not enforce media authorization.
security.serverValidation: Authorize every listed and submitted media ID, constrain signed URL lifetime and scope, and revalidate ownership before persistence.
security.secretPolicy: Never provide storage credentials, signing secrets, or privileged provider tokens to the picker.
security.externalNavigation: Composer renders thumbnails but performs no external navigation; constrain remote image origins in the project.
warnings: Media IDs and URLs received from the browser are untrusted.
Client-visible catalogs can leak filenames, metadata, or assets even when selection is disabled.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable upload queue behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for storage, delivery, transformation, or editorial policy owned by a project adapter.
guidance.responsive: Keep controls touch accessible and media within the viewport without assuming a fixed aspect ratio.

title: Media Field
replaces: combined URL upload and media field

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable media field behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for storage, delivery, transformation, or editorial policy owned by a project adapter.
guidance.responsive: Keep controls touch accessible and media within the viewport without assuming a fixed aspect ratio.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable relation select behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for display-only values or when a native control already satisfies the complete project contract.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels, errors, and controls readable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable sortable list behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when project-specific business behavior would make the shared data contract misleading.
guidance.responsive: Preserve reading order and keyboard access; contain intentional overflow inside the component boundary.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable image cropper behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for storage, delivery, transformation, or editorial policy owned by a project adapter.
guidance.responsive: Keep controls touch accessible and media within the viewport without assuming a fixed aspect ratio.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable document preview behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for storage, delivery, transformation, or editorial policy owned by a project adapter.
guidance.responsive: Keep controls touch accessible and media within the viewport without assuming a fixed aspect ratio.

title: Audio Player
accessibilityChecks: captions or transcript tracks are supported

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable audio player behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for storage, delivery, transformation, or editorial policy owned by a project adapter.
guidance.responsive: Keep controls touch accessible and media within the viewport without assuming a fixed aspect ratio.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable bulk action bar behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when another Composer action primitive more accurately communicates the interaction.
guidance.responsive: Maintain an accessible target size and allow labels or groups to wrap without clipping.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable status select behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for display-only values or when a native control already satisfies the complete project contract.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels, errors, and controls readable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable filter chip behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when the interaction is not a user choice or does not need selection and form semantics.
guidance.responsive: Allow choices to wrap or stack while preserving their native group semantics.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable resource toolbar behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when project-specific business behavior would make the shared data contract misleading.
guidance.responsive: Preserve reading order and keyboard access; contain intentional overflow inside the component boundary.

guidance.use: Use for a project-parsed tabular import review with a selected file, explicit row errors, preview, reset, and deliberate commit action.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for direct file uploads, binary formats without a tabular preview, or imports whose mapping and validation require a specialized project workflow.
guidance.responsive: Keep the file control and commit action reachable and contain the preview table as intentional overflow at narrow widths.

decision.nativeAlternative: FileUpload plus project validation is simpler when no preview or commit checkpoint is needed.
decision.complexAlternative: Use a project import wizard for column mapping, multi-stage validation, background jobs, rollback, or partial-commit policy.
decision.stateModel: The project owns selected File, parsed rows, errors, pending state, reset, and commit callbacks.
decision.serverClientBoundary: Client boundary required for File values and action callbacks; parsing can occur in a worker or on the server under project policy.
decision.serialization: Do not serialize File or arbitrary React preview nodes; send the original file or normalized project records through an explicit transport.
decision.focusOwnership: The native file input owns selection focus; the project should move focus to the error summary after failed validation.
failureModes: Committing previewed client rows without server revalidation permits tampering.
Rendering unbounded previews can freeze the browser.
Treating CSV cells as formulas when exporting later can create spreadsheet injection.

file.description: Browser File selected by the project; it is not serializable across a Server Component boundary.

rows.description: Bounded preview rows only. The server must parse and validate the authoritative import independently.

onCommit.description: Signals project-owned commit intent; Composer performs no upload, database write, or transaction.

runtime.listenerModel: Native file input plus reset and commit callbacks; no global listeners.
runtime.scaleGuidance: Preview a bounded sample and move large parsing or imports to a worker or server job.

security.htmlPolicy: Preview cells are React nodes supplied by the project; never construct them from unsanitized HTML.
security.clientValidation: The preview and error list are not authoritative validation and can be modified in the browser.
security.serverValidation: Reparse the original file, enforce authorization, validate every row and column, cap file and row counts, and use transactional or idempotent commit rules.
security.secretPolicy: Never put database credentials, storage secrets, or privileged import tokens in the file, preview rows, or callbacks.
security.commerceAuthority: Imported prices, inventory, orders, or discounts require domain-specific server validation and authorization.
warnings: Client preview rows must never be the authoritative import payload.
CSV and spreadsheet content can contain formulas, oversized fields, malicious encodings, or unexpected delimiters.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable audit log behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when project-specific business behavior would make the shared data contract misleading.
guidance.responsive: Preserve reading order and keyboard access; contain intentional overflow inside the component boundary.

title: Inline Edit
accessibilityChecks: focus moves into and returns from editing

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable inline edit behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when project-specific business behavior would make the shared data contract misleading.
guidance.responsive: Preserve reading order and keyboard access; contain intentional overflow inside the component boundary.

title: Phone Input

id.description: Stable ID for the telephone input and FormSummary error links.

countries.description: Optional allowed country choices with ISO code and display label.

name.description: Native form field name; a hidden input submits the normalized E.164 value.

accessibilityChecks: country and number controls are labeled

selectionHints.message: Use PhoneInput instead of generic Input type="tel" so normalization, country selection, form submission, and errors share the Composer contract.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable phone input behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for display-only values or when a native control already satisfies the complete project contract.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels, errors, and controls readable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.

title: Currency Input
accessibilityChecks: currency is included in the accessible name

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable currency input behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for display-only values or when a native control already satisfies the complete project contract.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels, errors, and controls readable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.

title: Duration Input
accessibilityChecks: normalized duration is available to forms

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable duration input behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for display-only values or when a native control already satisfies the complete project contract.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels, errors, and controls readable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.

accessibilityChecks: date and time controls share a group label

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable date time picker behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for display-only values or when a native control already satisfies the complete project contract.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels, errors, and controls readable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable timezone select behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for display-only values or when a native control already satisfies the complete project contract.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels, errors, and controls readable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.

title: Combobox Multi Select
accessibilityChecks: selected options have named remove actions

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable combobox multi select behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for display-only values or when a native control already satisfies the complete project contract.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels, errors, and controls readable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.

title: Field Array
accessibilityChecks: add and remove actions are named

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable field array behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for display-only values or when a native control already satisfies the complete project contract.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels, errors, and controls readable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable form summary behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when the message is ordinary page content rather than state or outcome feedback.
guidance.responsive: Allow long and translated messages to wrap without separating them from related actions.

title: Honeypot Field
accessibilityChecks: field is excluded from assistive technology
field is removed from keyboard order

guidance.use: Use as one low-friction bot signal inside a form when the server explicitly checks the field and combines it with rate limiting and abuse controls.
guidance.avoid: Avoid presenting it as spam protection by itself or relying on hidden client behavior as an authorization or validation boundary.
guidance.responsive: The field remains inert and unavailable to assistive technology; verify project CSS does not accidentally reveal or focus it.

decision.nativeAlternative: A project-owned visually hidden decoy field is equivalent when its server contract is documented and tested.
decision.complexAlternative: Use layered project abuse protection such as rate limits, behavioral signals, proof-of-work, or a privacy-reviewed challenge service.
decision.stateModel: Uncontrolled native text input that should remain empty for legitimate users.
decision.serverClientBoundary: Server-compatible presentation, but only a server handler can interpret the submitted value as an abuse signal.
decision.serialization: Submits a named text field through FormData; the server must know the exact configured name.
decision.focusOwnership: The inert container removes the field from focus and accessibility trees.
failureModes: Ignoring the field on the server makes it inert markup with no abuse value.
Rejecting every filled value without layered signals can block password managers or assistive tooling.
Treating the honeypot as sufficient protection leaves automated submissions largely unmitigated.

name.description: FormData key the server must check. Keep the configured name synchronized with server validation.

runtime.scaleGuidance: One field per form is sufficient; additional decoys do not replace rate limiting.

security.htmlPolicy: Label text is escaped by React.
security.clientValidation: The hidden field is only a signal and provides no client-side security.
security.serverValidation: Check the exact field name on the server and combine the result with rate limiting, logging, and project abuse policy.
security.secretPolicy: Do not encode secret detection rules or privileged tokens in the field name or markup.
warnings: A honeypot is not a substitute for server-side validation, authorization, throttling, or abuse monitoring.
Automated clients can detect and leave conventional honeypot fields empty.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable search input behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for display-only values or when a native control already satisfies the complete project contract.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels, errors, and controls readable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.

title: Facet Filter
accessibilityChecks: counts are not the only option names

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable facet filter behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when the interaction is not a user choice or does not need selection and form semantics.
guidance.responsive: Allow choices to wrap or stack while preserving their native group semantics.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable active filters behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when project-specific business behavior would make the shared data contract misleading.
guidance.responsive: Preserve reading order and keyboard access; contain intentional overflow inside the component boundary.

title: Search Results Summary
accessibilityChecks: query and count are expressed in text

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable search results summary behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when project-specific business behavior would make the shared data contract misleading.
guidance.responsive: Preserve reading order and keyboard access; contain intentional overflow inside the component boundary.

title: Quantity Input
accessibilityChecks: increment and decrement actions are named

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable quantity input behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for display-only values or when a native control already satisfies the complete project contract.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels, errors, and controls readable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.

title: Money
accessibilityChecks: sale and original values are distinguishable

selectionHints.message: Pass integer currency values through Money.valueMinor instead of dividing by 100 into the legacy major-unit value prop.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable money behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for project typography or static semantic content that does not require shared behavior.
guidance.responsive: Keep authoring and preview controls usable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.

title: Currency Select
accessibilityChecks: currency code and name are available

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable currency select behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for display-only values or when a native control already satisfies the complete project contract.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels, errors, and controls readable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.

title: Product Option Select
accessibilityChecks: unavailable options are disabled and described

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable product option select behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when the interaction is not a user choice or does not need selection and form semantics.
guidance.responsive: Allow choices to wrap or stack while preserving their native group semantics.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable cart line behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when project-specific business behavior would make the shared data contract misleading.
guidance.responsive: Preserve reading order and keyboard access; contain intentional overflow inside the component boundary.

guidance.use: Use to present project-calculated cart or order totals with semantic labels, a final total, optional note, and rendered checkout actions.
guidance.avoid: Avoid computing prices, discounts, tax, shipping, inventory, payment status, or order authority inside the component.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels and values associated, allow long localized amounts and notes to wrap, and keep checkout actions reachable.

decision.nativeAlternative: A semantic section with a description list is sufficient for a one-off summary.
decision.complexAlternative: Use a project checkout summary when live shipping, tax, payment, financing, or jurisdiction-specific disclosures require domain orchestration.
decision.stateModel: Pure caller-owned presentation with no internal pricing or mutation state.
decision.serverClientBoundary: Server-compatible when rows and rendered values are serializable; interactive actions may introduce a project client boundary.
decision.serialization: Pass already-formatted rendered values or Money components backed by integer minor units; never derive authoritative totals from display strings.
decision.focusOwnership: Project-owned actions own focus; the summary itself introduces no focus management.
failureModes: Trusting browser-calculated totals permits price and discount tampering.
Using floating-point major units creates rounding errors.
Displaying stale inventory or tax state as final misleads checkout users.

rows.description: Presentation rows only; labels and rendered values must come from project-authorized pricing state.

total.description: Rendered total display. It is not parsed, calculated, or treated as payment authority.

actions.description: Rendered project checkout actions; Composer performs no checkout or payment request.

runtime.listenerModel: No listeners in Composer; rendered project actions may be interactive.
runtime.scaleGuidance: Use for concise summaries, not itemized ledgers or long invoice histories.

security.htmlPolicy: Labels, values, notes, and actions are escaped React content unless the project deliberately supplies a different renderer.
security.clientValidation: Displayed totals and disabled actions do not prove pricing, inventory, or checkout validity.
security.serverValidation: Recalculate every amount from authoritative catalog, discount, tax, shipping, customer, and inventory data before creating a payment or order.
security.secretPolicy: Never render payment credentials, provider secrets, private order tokens, or full sensitive payment data.
security.externalNavigation: Project actions own checkout navigation and must validate return and redirect destinations.
security.commerceAuthority: Presentation only; the server and payment provider own monetary and order authority.
warnings: Browser-visible totals are untrusted input at the server boundary.
Money values should use integer minor units and explicit currency.

title: Discount Code Input
accessibilityChecks: apply and remove actions are named

guidance.use: Use for collecting, applying, displaying, and removing a discount code through project-owned callbacks with explicit pending and feedback states.
guidance.avoid: Avoid embedding discount eligibility, value calculation, stacking rules, or authorization in the browser.
guidance.responsive: Keep the label, input, apply or remove action, and feedback readable and reachable at narrow widths.

decision.nativeAlternative: A labeled Input and Button are simpler when applied-code and pending feedback do not need a shared contract.
decision.complexAlternative: Use a project promotion surface for automatic offers, multiple codes, gift cards, account entitlements, or jurisdiction-specific rules.
decision.stateModel: Input text may be controlled or initialized by defaultValue; appliedCode, pending, success, and error remain caller-owned.
decision.serverClientBoundary: Client boundary required for input and submit state; validation and discount calculation must execute through a project server boundary.
decision.serialization: onApply receives a trimmed string only; the server must normalize it and return authoritative cart state.
decision.focusOwnership: The input and buttons own focus; feedback is announced through status or alert roles.
failureModes: Calculating discounts in the browser permits price manipulation.
Returning detailed rejection reasons can disclose private promotion rules.
Allowing duplicate pending submissions creates race conditions in cart state.

onApply.description: Receives the trimmed candidate code. The project must validate it on the server and replace cart state with the authoritative result.

appliedCode.description: Caller-authoritative display value for the currently applied code.

pending.description: Caller-owned mutation state used to block duplicate apply or remove actions.

runtime.listenerModel: One local form submit, input change, and optional remove callback.
runtime.scaleGuidance: Designed for one code at a time; use a project promotion manager for multiple instruments.

security.htmlPolicy: Code, success, and error text render as escaped content.
security.clientValidation: Trimming, pending state, and disabled buttons do not validate discount eligibility.
security.serverValidation: Normalize the code, authorize the cart and customer, enforce eligibility and stacking rules, rate limit guesses, and recalculate authoritative totals.
security.secretPolicy: Do not expose private promotion rules, generation secrets, or internal campaign identifiers in client errors.
security.commerceAuthority: The server owns code validity, discount value, eligibility, and final totals.
warnings: Verbose invalid-code responses can enable promotion enumeration.

title: Price Range Input
accessibilityChecks: slider and numeric values stay synchronized

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable price range input behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for display-only values or when a native control already satisfies the complete project contract.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels, errors, and controls readable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.
